focus: Proactive avoidance, situational awareness, and multi-attacker threat recognition integrated with combat technique evidence

voters.name: Combat Sport Empiricist
voters.trusts: Full-contact outcomes, sparring transfer, fight-finishing technique data
voters.distrusts: Awareness seminars without physical validation, compliance drills

voters.name: Operational Pragmatist
voters.trusts: LE/military adoption, NCVS outcomes, EAAA RCT, field injury reduction
voters.distrusts: Gym marketing, untested Krav schools, theoretical SA without drills

voters.name: Evidence Skeptic
voters.trusts: RCTs, replicated cohort studies, transparent methodology
voters.distrusts: Missing metrics presented as facts, Cooper Color Code without outcome data, 90% ground myth

criteria.name: Proactive Threat Detection & Avoidance
criteria.description: Ability to sense unfolding danger and exit before engagement — the missing metric proxy

criteria.name: Multi-Attacker Escape Architecture
criteria.description: Premeditated tactics to avoid being drawn into numerical disadvantage

criteria.name: Situational Awareness / Vigilance Training Evidence
criteria.description: Documented improvement in perception, risk assessment, decision speed

criteria.name: Stress Transfer Under Live Resistance
criteria.description: Techniques survive adrenaline and resisting opponents

criteria.name: Measured Victimization Outcome Reduction
criteria.description: Hardest standard — did victimization or injury actually decrease?

interventions: Stay Alert Stay Alive + Cooper SA Doctrine

EAAA / Empowerment Self-Defense.verdict: Only intervention with RCT-proven victimization reduction. Assess unit directly trains danger detection. No direct multi-attacker avoidance RCT — V3 penalty applied.

Stay Alert Stay Alive + Cooper SA Doctri.verdict: Correct philosophy (avoidance-first) but lacks civilian RCT outcome data. Military/LE institutional adoption is real; victimization metrics are not.

Integrated MMA (Boxing + Wrestling + Mua.verdict: Supreme when fight is unavoidable. Actively harmful as primary SD strategy — trains engagement, not avoidance. V2/V3 slash proactive scores.

Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.verdict: Tier A for control/restraint. Tier F for multi-attacker proactive avoidance. Ground-first = drawn into worst scenario.

Boxing + Wrestling (no ground submission.verdict: Best combat-sport stack without ground-entanglement risk. Early KO data supports standup. No victimization RCT.

Krav Maga.verdict: Scenario scripting addresses multi-attacker cognitively — but V3 vetoes without outcome data or live resistance standard.

Traditional Martial Arts (low-contact).verdict: Unanimous last place. No proactive avoidance evidence. No combat transfer.

adversarialFindings.finding: The missing metric — proactive multi-attacker avoidance — cannot be filled by any martial art RCT. It requires near-miss reporting infrastructure that does not exist in NCVS/UCR.
EAAA 'Assess' unit is the closest validated proxy for 'sensed danger → took action before assault completed' with 50-57% victimization reduction.
Stay Alert Stay Alive / Cooper Color Code are doctrinally correct but evidence-tier C for civilian outcomes. Use as framework, not proof.
When outnumbered and already engaged, escape (22%) beats fighting (11%). Premeditated avoidance is logically superior but unmeasured.
Optimal stack: SA doctrine (C) + EAAA Assess/Acknowledge (A+) + Boxing/Wrestling standup (A) + BJJ escapes not guard play (A) + LE control if professional role.

recommendedStack.component: Low kick + push kick for distance
